topic: A tenant receives an eviction notice
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp, official paper felt like sandpaper under my fingertips as I unfolded it on the worn kitchen table. Another notice. I let out a slow, deliberate breath, the air catching in my chest like a snagged thread. I stared at the highlighted section, my jaw tightening until a dull ache bloomed just beneath my ear. This was absurd. After five years of paying on time, after being a quiet, respectful resident, this sudden, aggressive move felt entirely unjustified. I crumpled the paper once, then smoothed it out again, my eyes scanning the fine print with a practiced, resentful scrutiny. I pushed the stack aside with unnecessary force, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet apartment.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" I muttered under my breath, the sound raspy and low. I paced the length of the living room three times, my boots clicking sharply on the hardwood floor. My hands clenched into fists, the knuckles turning white against my palms. The letter sat on the counter, a stark white accusation against the dull grey of the laminate. I picked up my phone, not to call anyone, but just to feel the cool glass in my hand, a brief, sharp distraction from the churning in my stomach. I reread the dates, the legal jargon blurring into meaningless noise. I dropped the phone onto the sofa cushion with a heavy thud, the sheer unfairness of the situation making my shoulders slump with heavy defeat.

[story 3]

He picked up the envelope, his gaze lingering on the date stamped across the top. A muscle ticked visibly in his jaw. He didn't immediately open it; instead, he turned it over in his hands, studying the thick, impersonal font. A low, almost inaudible sigh escaped him, heavy with suppressed frustration. He finally tore it open, the sound surprisingly loud in the small hallway. His eyes swept over the demands, and a visible tightening around his mouth suggested a deep internal resistance. He folded the notice with meticulous, almost angry precision, tucking it deep into his briefcase, the movement stiff and controlled.
